sentences.text: Oh my god.
This looks crazy.
Hey, Catherine.
How are you today?
I'm fine, how are you?
I am good, I am good.
Just switching to my headphones real quick.
Okay.
questions.
I shot him, like, a.
quick note back with general answers, but … Yeah, I saw that.
Okay, cool.
And then Sam sent me that question on what we do, I guess, to smooth out the data.
Unfortunately, I know that answer to be not a lot.
I was hoping that I would get something back in IQOps that maybe was a more elegant way of kind of telling them that.
So I'm just gonna kick that one down the curb and tell him that I'll respond to him via email, like, maybe later today or tomorrow when I hear back.
Sound good?
Okay, that sounds good, yeah.
Anything you want out of this, meeting, besides just a general, like, how's everything going?
No, I think I just wanted to be here so they don't think I'm… Ghost.
Binning them.
Fair enough.
Alright, it looks like I've got 5 in the waiting room, 4 in the waiting room.
I'm gonna let them in.
Great!
Hey, Amanda, Todd?
Good morning.
Good morning.
Morning.
Good morning!
Thinking maybe we're waiting on a few folks, Todd?
Here comes Sam.
Yeah, although I think a lot of people said they didn't have the meeting initially, … Amy?
Alright, fair enough.
Next time I'll… I guess I… I must have not added everyone to this note.
Yeah, I think Sam forwarded to everyone else.
Yeah, I didn't rea- hey guys, I didn't realize it was only me and Todd.
Apologies on our end for that, Sam.
Couldn't have picked the worst two people.
I'm just kidding.
You know what's funny?
I thought we were joining your meeting, I think maybe I misunderstood as well.
I was even surprised to see my own Zoom account.
Every Monday we meet from 8.
I just figured that once… one Monday a month, I'll just shrink that to 8.
Wow.
Right.
We'll join yours at night.
Excellent.
Is there anybody else we think we're waiting for, or do we want to kick it off right about now?
Should we give folks another minute or so?
What do you think?
No, let's kick it off.
I already heard from Danny and Mariela, they couldn't make it.
I don't see, Don.
But I see Raina… I know Ellen looks like she's out.
Raina, do you know if Don's out?
I… be joining.
Let me go ahead and message him.
I also had trouble getting in, too, so I don't know if anyone else did, but….
Yeah, that was the same case for me.
Okay, so maybe he's still.
Huh.
Alright, I'll keep an eye on our participants list and make sure no one gets stuck in the… in the waiting room, kind of.
But, let's jump in, yeah.
So, Todd, thank you so much for sending that list of questions over.
For everyone else, I wanted to kind of walk through some of those questions, because they're probably pretty common questions I imagine everybody has.
One of the first questions was, how do we, curate our properties tab?
Wondering.
Is that something everybody's kind of feeling, where we're seeing everything all at once?
I definitely want to walk through that.
So, my agenda here, just walk through some of those questions.
Is there anything else top of mind, Sam, the group that I should be, I should be kind of covering today, or should we stick, to those couple of questions that Todd had sent over?
Yeah, I threw one in there, and then, all the thoughts, questions.
Other than that, people can chime in as needed.
… We can maybe handle this, likely handle it internally, but we also have a new hire.
Amanda Williams will be heading up our East Coast portfolio.
She joined the call, might be good for, like, a quick training, like a user interface training.
… like I said, we could likely handle that internally, but if you… if there's, like, at least a 30-minute spot to do it separately with Amanda, that would be… that'd be great.
Definitely, yeah.
Amanda, I'll reach out to you, great to get connected, I'll reach out to you.
My schedule's kind of funny this week, because I am out Wednesday, Thursday, Friday for a wedding in Mexico, so really excited for that.
Oh, you poor thing.
Yeah, that's terrible.
So sorry.
Gotta sip a few Mai Tais and celebrate this week, but my availability next week is gonna be great.
I'll send you some times, if that works, alright?
Perfect, thank you.
Sure thing.
Alright, so then let's get started.
I will share my screen quickly, and we'll look at ApartmentIQ.
And the first question was, maybe, how do we curate this My Properties list so that it's not so busy?
What's really lovely is that we can come right in here to the Manage Users tab.
You'll all have that functionality here.
Anybody who's an admin can make full changes to what folks are seeing.
If you are an admin, the crux of that is, by default, you see everything in the tool.
So you'll notice here, access is locked, because that's part of your rights as an administrative user.
But everybody else, perhaps Alec, for instance, if we wanted to change their access.
Most folks have everything selected by default.
But we can go ahead and cut this list down so that you can only see the properties that are most relevant, or most remain to you in your dashboard.
Hopefully that helps cut down some of the clutter.
If anybody's having any issues there, I'm happy to have our team take care of this on your behalf.
If you want to just shoot me a note with the properties that you want to see, I can get that taken care of for you.
Fair enough.
Yep, sounds good, thanks.
Sure.
Todd had another question about adding, or rather, editing a market survey to add a floor plan that doesn't exist maybe at the subject property, but may exist at one of the comps.
One of the examples he used is the emblem coir, so I'm gonna go ahead and find emblem and walk through that exactly.
So, Among Conyers.
It sounds like we have, one… only one and two bed floor plans there, and we might want to run a report to see what our comps that have studios and three beds are offering.
So how can we expand the scope of the reporting In here.
One way to accomplish that would be to use these filters here at the top left-hand corner.
It allows us to filter out for anything that doesn't match these exact set of parameters.
So, if we want to just see, studios, we would start in our Bedrooms filter, enable studios.
And if we wanted to see, 3 beds, we would do the same here.
Looks like Don's joining now.
And then you'd go ahead and hit edit.
Apply, excuse me.
And that would actually change all the existing data in your comp set to just reflect anything that matches a studio or 3-bed in this case.
So all the availability, even, is reflecting there.
I'm gonna go ahead and make sure we have all our metrics turned on so that we get the totality of the experience here.
And so that's what will happen here.
You'll find all the availability, vacancy.
For instance, we can click right into this and see exactly what the floor plans are.
In this case, at the Conyers, there's only 3 beds.
But we can see… Maybe another property?
If they have studios.
This looks like a lot of 3-beds as well.
Okay, so here we have a few studios, floor plan S1, S1, S1.
As well as some 3-beds.
And now, if we wanted to move into our floor plan, Report?
We can go ahead and move through here.
Oh, why do we have no… That's interesting.
Nothing the last 6 months.
Still nothing.
So the good news, or rather, the way we should expect ApartmentIQ to behave, is that it's going to respect the filters that you have enabled.
So anything that matches the studio or 3-bed will show up here.
And will show up as you continue through ApartmentIQ.
Respect all the filters you have.
Not sure why we're getting this error here, but definitely something I will be logging and, keeping up with to make sure that this works the way we want it to.
After the margaritas at the wedding.
Yeah, Sam, maybe that's it.
Maybe somebody got into the margaritas already this week, and that's why we're not working yet.
Todd, does this kind of address what you were looking for, or is this not quite it?

Well, it definitely does, but it does leave me to ask, I guess, so if you were to make this filter change, so then if you were to log out of DepartmentIQ and come back in, is this going to be my default market survey for Conyers going forward?
It will not.
That's a great question, and it's actually something we're actively working on, this idea of kind of a saved view or perspectives.
I think other platforms refer to it that way.

It's something we have on our roadmap, something we're looking to prioritize, I think, before the end of the year, if not sooner, but I'm gonna go ahead and add your name to the list of requesters and see if that makes a change.
But with something we're aware of, we know that we want this to be customized for you.
We've started and created a new area in the My Settings This is the start, for instance, and lays the groundwork for us to create additional fields here that you can set as your defaults.
The first iteration might be a default that is ApartmentIQ-wide.

So, for instance, if you set a specific set of parameters, that'll be the view across every single market survey, but ideally, we do want to be in a place where it is specific to the market surveys that are most relevant for you.

Something that can help is making sure that your cookies do not auto-delete, because if your cookies are set to not auto-delete, it will respect the customized metrics that you have enabled every single time, not the filters, though.
So, apologies for the caveat there, but it's something we're working toward in the next couple months.
That's fine.
And then, I guess, somewhat related, so on, like, scheduled reports, right?
So we have it set up to send everything on Monday.
I'm assuming that whenever you schedule the report, that's the version it's always going to send?
Is that going to be affected at all by any of these changes, or… you know, you know what I'm asking, I guess.
Yeah, I hear, yeah, and we were really excited when we launched the configurable reporting.
That was a really big step forward for us, but the crux of that is that the way it works is it's going to respect how you had your market survey configured on the day you scheduled it.
So, if I came in and created a scheduled report for Avenir, and I had XYZ configuration, and then hit my schedule.
To schedule this reporting.
It's going to send this report the way I have it scheduled today, it's going to send it every single time.
let's say tomorrow ApartmentIQ comes out with a new metric.
For instance, we recently lost, launched a, losses, cancels metric.
So, that will no longer be included because that metric maybe came out tomorrow, and so you'd have to redo this reporting.
So, that's kind of a quality of life fix that we've also, keyed in on.
You're not the first person to point that out, so additional kinds of improvements coming as we launch new things.
Okay, nice.
Sure.
… Alright, I think, the last thing that I wanted to note here on the questions that Todd had sent over was the limited data.
I think we saw that at the last market survey we were hanging out in.
Let me see if I can go back to it.
If anyone remembers what that was… here we go.
Alright.
So, this limited data tag, and what does it represent?
ApartmentIQ, as we probably all know, is scraping the websites every day.
We're scraping the internet to make sure that we are pulling in the most up-to-date information.
What can happen is that websites change, behavior on how the websites feed a web… on how the feed is being delivered can change.
And that behavior can set off certain red flags on our side.

So if I, ApartmentIQ, sees a website behaving bizarrely, maybe there's sudden availability being added or removed in a small period of time, maybe pricing is changing really, really rapidly, things that might reflect IT issues, perhaps, or just changes in maybe… branding or management at the website.
These are all the kinds of things that can create this limited data flag.
It basically is our way of saying, hey.
We've seen some strange behavior at this website that gives us pause when going ahead and calculating these metrics for you, so we just want to bring that to your attention.

You are still able to see The information that we are collecting, but we kind of want to put a little asterisk there and say, maybe give it an extra look or a little bit of extra scrutiny here as you're evaluating these metrics, to make sure that things are on the up and up.

Apartment IQ would rather be a little more conservative, so some of these flags can maybe… I don't want to say we over-rotate on them, but we'd rather be a little more conservative and make sure you pay just that extra little bit of attention, kind of that grain of salt, just in case something is a little funky.
But you're still able to go ahead and you can click… this is supposed to be a little eyeball icon.
You can click that, and it'll show you the metrics that we are collecting.
You're still able to interact with everything and click in and see what we're collecting here.
You'll notice in this case, the reason for the data tag is that we believe they are likely limiting their advertised occupancy, which can be contributing to some funky calculations.
What do you… I think this is helpful, I think this was maybe, like, a… somewhat of a smaller percentage of the question I put in there.
More of… how do you go back and smooth out data in a time series standpoint, is what I'm probably more concerned with.
So, for example, half of our whole East Coast portfolio right now is transitioning in real-page instances.
Based on the merger that we had.
So I think, if I got this correctly, we're… we're showing every unit being available with no rents being posted.
… Call it for a week, for simplicity.
Sure.
Now, once the data starts coming in properly, starting here in the next 2 days, what do you all do for those 10 days, 7 to 10 days, in terms of a timeline series, whether it's occupancy or rents?
That's a great question, Sam, and I wanted to have a more fleshed-out answer for you by now.
Unfortunately, I haven't quite.
We gave you… we gave you a whopping 30 minutes, I'm surprised.
But that is, that is, like….
Absolutely.

It just brought to our mind because of what's going on for us, but if it just comes up, … for other… deals, I'm just curious on how you smooth out data, because I have seen where it says limited data quite often, but then I wonder what… it just got me thinking of what do you all do to help clean up some time series data?
Right, so I think that's… you're right in that it's two parts.
The limited data tag is more so a live time, hey, this is what we think is happening right now, we're seeing look out for this data, give it another run-through.
For the historical aspect, where, hey, there was a transition, maybe, the website was down.
The historical aspect, we have something automated in the back to check for NAs, for instance, when zero rents are pulling through.
As far as smoothing things out.
it would have to be a bit of a manual pass right now.

I was hoping maybe in the case of your East Coast portfolio, if it's as simple as me just taking a look at all the properties that are on the East Coast, I'm happy to run those through the microscope with our data team and just see what they can do.
Yeah, wait a few days.
If you could wait a few days for it to come back online, and then your hangover from Mexico, resolves.
Sam, you are so considerate.
No, but just, wait a- wait a couple days, though.
For it to get back online, because then… then you could have the, kind of, that gap where you can… take a look at.

Absolutely, and I'll get you the official word from our engineering resources later today, when they kind of start waking up and taking a look at the questions we've posted for the day, to let you know what we officially do on that smoothing process, but in the short term, and just for the sake of keeping your data clean over the next couple weeks, I'll go ahead and have them take a special look at the East Coast portfolio, in the next few days.
Maybe we'll call it Thursday, Friday, I'll have them take a look, or better to start next week?
Yeah, Thursday, Friday would be good.
All right, yeah.
And I'll share that, I'll reply to everybody on that thread so everyone can see the answer.
I apologize again for not having that sooner.
It is something that has come up in the past, so we want to be sure to Speak to that eloquently and elegantly.
… that kind of runs through… I think we've talked about all the questions I got from Todd and yourself.
Anything else on the minds of folks as we engage with the tool?
How are we feeling?
general feedback.
Not for this call, maybe even not for next one, but just at some point, I think you all… were you all working on a rent growth forecast tool, or no?
I'm trying to remember.
You were, right?
Okay.
Yeah, I would like to revisit that at a later date.
I think we've talked about this plenty, though, but, like, that's what we're using a marker for, for the rent growth forecast and their demographic information.
You know, we would like to revisit that at some point down the line, as well.
Yeah, that's right in Catherine's wheelhouse.
You're nodding very aggressively.
Yeah, we… we have had a couple delays in releasing that, but should be out in September.
Oh, okay, okay.
So, that should line up perfectly if you're wanting to wait a bit to have that conversation anyway.
Sounds good.
Excellent.

Bill, I think on the, I guess, the townhomes questions, because, like, for example, we have, like, Emblem Riverside, and, you know, like, we have two-bedroom townhomes and flats, and it kind of blends together, and so it's not the most relevant of numbers, and I just think it's a way to create, you know, separate reports.
What we can maybe… Unfortunately, not separate reports.
I apologize, you're right, that was the last… I've read right through that one.
Sometimes, through a combination of these filters, we can find a way to omit specific floor plans.
For instance, townhomes, I know something a lot of folks have done in the past, is using sizing or even rent ranges to kind of exclude some properties or some unit types.
from a given analysis, so if we talk about a town… I'll tell you where this happened often with penthouses.
Before, we had the unit style to either exclude or omit penthouses.
We used to recommend, hey, your penthouses, 2, 3, 4 bedrooms, you set the bedroom and bathroom combo that you like.
Then you create a sizing floor.
Because, as we all know, the penthouse two-bedroom is gonna be way bigger than the, standard 2-bedroom apartments.
So, that was one way we used to kind of work around.
I'm not 100% sure if the townhouses are gonna… work in the same way.
Todd, how does that sound?
Maybe creating a bedroom-bathroom combo with a given size range that qualifies?
Yeah, I should be able to try that again.
The difference is, is really the comp set, though, typically, right?

Because, like, we have, you know… I mean, I would probably need to create a report, set up for schedule, and then kind of go back and reset my default one, because Yeah, the comms we're pulling in to look at the townhome, you know, we have some that are purely 100% townhome communities that we're copping to, that I wouldn't.
Yeah, whistle.
want to have in the overall report.
So, I mean, yeah, there's probably a workaround there.
But yes….
Do you have… are you using the research report functionality?
Yeah.
Yes.
I see it.

Okay, so is there a world where you would create a research report for this property, maybe, that comps to the… that given set of properties versus maybe the the My Properties version comping to the other set of properties?
Is that kind of….
Yeah, I can make better than you.
a workaround.
Let me know how that works out for you, and then, you know, it sounds like that's… you've got a couple of unique situations here.
If that doesn't quite work out.
Sounds like it'd be really cool to get you in front of our product team and just kind of walk them through, let them hear how you'd like ApartmentIQ to work out for some of these properties.
That's really how we like to build our roadmap as well.
So, if it's something you need, it's probably something folks need elsewhere.
Okay.
Yeah, I'll try that, and we can talk later.
Appreciate it, Todd.
And the same goes for anybody else.
If you're finding any hiccups as you work through Part 1IQ or little nuances that you'd like to see, please don't hesitate to let myself, Catherine, know.
We are very much a customer-driven product, so we're really excited to hear what you guys need and impact that in the tool.
If everybody else is feeling good, maybe we cut it here for today, and we'll talk to you guys soon.
Sam, I'll have that follow-up shortly when I hear back from our team, and … We'll talk in a month, I suppose, otherwise.
Guys, appreciate it.
Thank you.
Absolutely.
Thank you very much.
Thanks.
Take care.
Thank you.
